{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/21798","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/21798/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/21798/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/21798/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/21798","id":228007240,"node_id":"MDU6SXNzdWUyMjgwMDcyNDA=","number":21798,"title":"Strange behavior of Julia implicit multiplication","user":{"login":"ronisbr","id":1068295,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjEwNjgyOTU=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1068295?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/ronisbr","html_url":"https://github.com/ronisbr","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ronisbr/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ronisbr/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ronisbr/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ronisbr/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ronisbr/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ronisbr/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ronisbr/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ronisbr/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/ronisbr/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":9,"created_at":"2017-05-11T14:17:13Z","updated_at":"2024-10-11T20:41:17Z","closed_at":"2017-05-11T14:53:55Z","author_association":"MEMBER","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Hi guys!

I do not know if this was asked before, but I notice a very strange behavior with julia and the implicit multiplication. On the Internet, there has been a lot of conversation about the solution of the following equation:

    6/2(2+1)

A Cassio calculator often outputs 1, whether other calculators output 9. From my point of view, the convention states that between a number and a parenthesis, there is an implicit multiplication signal. Hence, we should get:

    6/2(2+1) = 6/2*(2+1) = 3*(2+1) = 3*3 = 9

However, if I type this very same equation in julia, I get:

    julia> 6/2(2+1)
    1.0

Because, as in the Cassio calculator, julia is solving 2(2+1) first as if it was a number. However, for such notation, you must always solve the left-most multiplication / division operation. Is it a real problem or am I missing something?

Notice that if I explicitly put the multiplication signal, then julia computes it fine:

    julia> 6/2*(2+1)
    9.0

Information about my system:

```
julia> versioninfo()
Julia Version 0.5.1
Commit 6445c82* (2017-03-05 13:25 UTC)
Platform Info:
  OS: Linux (x86_64-suse-linux)
  CPU: Intel(R) Xeon(R) CPU E5-2687W 0 @ 3.10GHz
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  BLAS: libopenblas (DYNAMIC_ARCH NO_AFFINITY Sandybridge)
  LAPACK: libopenblas_openmp.so.0
  LIBM: libopenlibm
  LLVM: libLLVM-3.7.1 (ORCJIT, sandybridge)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/21798/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/21798/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":"completed"},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2408094853","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/21798#issuecomment-2408094853","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/21798","id":2408094853,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6PiJyF","user":{"login":"stevejacobscronung","id":182132669,"node_id":"U_kgDOCtsfvQ","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/182132669?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevejacobscronung","html_url":"https://github.com/stevejacobscronung","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevejacobscronung/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevejacobscronung/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevejacobscronung/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevejacobscronung/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevejacobscronung/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevejacobscronung/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevejacobscronung/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevejacobscronung/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/stevejacobscronung/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-10-11T20:41:16Z","updated_at":"2024-10-11T20:41:16Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"That is some obscure convention, implicit multiplication, for some reasons some people stick to it. But I didn't find any sources of such a n exotic convention. So, the best way is to regard as bug and misleading the provided feature of higher priority of implicit multiplication and avoid it. By all accepted conventions, which are used in Wolfram, WolframAlpha, Google, there is no higher priority given for multiplication, whether it is implicit or explicit. So, the 
There is a discussion about this convention, but with no sources provided. So it mentions that such a convention is not adopted, and 
https://people.math.harvard.edu/~knill/pedagogy/ambiguity/index.html#:~:text=In%20this%20more%20sophisticated%20convention,like%20x%20*%20%2F%20or%20%C3%B7.
**In this more sophisticated convention, which is often used in
algebra, implicit multiplication is given higher priority than explicit
multiplication or explicit division,** in which those operations are written
explicitly with symbols like x * / or ÷. Under this more sophisticated
convention, the implicit multiplication in 2(2 + 2) is given higher
priority than the explicit division in 8÷2(2 + 2). In other words,
2(2+2) should be evaluated first. Doing so yields 8÷2(2 + 2) = 8÷8 =
1. By the same rule, many commenters argued that the expression 8 ÷ 2(4)
was not synonymous with 8÷2x4, because the parentheses demanded immediate
resolution, thus giving 8÷8 = 1 again.

This **convention** is very reasonable, and I agree that **the answer is 1
if we adhere to it**. But it is **not universally adopted**. The calculators
built into Google and WolframAlpha use the more elementary convention;
they make no distinction between implicit and explicit multiplication
when instructed to evaluate simple arithmetic expressions.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2408094853/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
